PVS1.name: Null variant in a gene where LOF is a known mechanism of disease
PVS1.description: Nonsense, frameshift, canonical splice site, initiation codon, single or multi-exon deletion in a gene where loss-of-function is a known mechanism of disease.

PS1.name: Same amino acid change as a previously established pathogenic variant
PS1.description: Same amino acid change as an established pathogenic variant regardless of nucleotide change.

PS2.name: De novo (confirmed) in a patient with disease and no family history
PS2.description: De novo (both maternity and paternity confirmed) in a patient with the disease and no family history.

PS3.name: Well-established functional studies show damaging effect
PS3.description: Well-established in vitro or in vivo functional studies supportive of a damaging effect on the gene or gene product.

PS4.name: Prevalence significantly increased in affected vs. controls
PS4.description: The prevalence of the variant in affected individuals is significantly increased compared with the prevalence in controls.

PM1.name: Located in a mutational hot spot or well-studied functional domain
PM1.description: Located in a mutational hot spot and/or critical and well-established functional domain without benign variation.

PM2.name: Absent from controls or extremely low frequency
PM2.description: Absent from controls (or at extremely low frequency if recessive) in population databases.

PM3.name: Detected in trans with a pathogenic variant for recessive disorders
PM3.description: For recessive disorders, detected in trans with a pathogenic variant.

PM4.name: Protein length change due to in-frame deletion/insertion
PM4.description: Protein length changes as a result of in-frame deletions/insertions in a non-repeat region or stop-loss variants.

PM5.name: Novel missense at a residue where a different pathogenic missense has been seen
PM5.description: Novel missense change at an amino acid residue where a different missense change determined to be pathogenic has been seen before.

PM6.name: Assumed de novo without confirmation of paternity/maternity
PM6.description: Assumed de novo, but without confirmation of paternity and maternity.

PP1.name: Cosegregation with disease in multiple family members
PP1.description: Cosegregation with disease in multiple affected family members in a gene definitively known to cause the disease.

PP2.name: Missense in a gene with low rate of benign missense and pathogenic missenses are common
PP2.description: Missense variant in a gene that has a low rate of benign missense variation and in which missense variants are a common mechanism of disease.

PP3.name: Multiple computational tools predict deleterious effect
PP3.description: Multiple lines of computational evidence support a deleterious effect on the gene or gene product.

PP4.name: Patient phenotype highly specific for gene-associated disease
PP4.description: Patient's phenotype or family history is highly specific for a disease with a single genetic etiology.

PP5.name: Reputable source reports variant as pathogenic
PP5.description: Reputable source recently reports variant as pathogenic, but evidence is not available to the laboratory to perform an independent evaluation.

BA1.name: Allele frequency >5% in any general population dataset
BA1.description: Allele frequency is >5% in Exome Sequencing Project, 1000 Genomes Project, or Exome Aggregation Consortium.

BS1.name: Allele frequency greater than expected for disorder
BS1.description: Allele frequency is greater than expected for disorder.

BS2.name: Observed in healthy adult with full penetrance expected at early age
BS2.description: Observed in a healthy adult individual for a recessive (homozygous), dominant (heterozygous), or X-linked (hemizygous) disorder, with full penetrance expected at an early age.

BS3.name: Well-established functional studies show no damaging effect
BS3.description: Well-established in vitro or in vivo functional studies show no damaging effect on protein function or splicing.

BS4.name: Lack of segregation in affected family members
BS4.description: Lack of segregation in affected members of a family.

BP1.name: Missense in a gene where only truncating variants cause disease
BP1.description: Missense variant in a gene for which primarily truncating variants are known to cause disease.

BP2.name: Observed in trans or in cis with a pathogenic variant
BP2.description: Observed in trans with a pathogenic variant for a fully penetrant dominant gene/disorder or observed in cis with a pathogenic variant in any inheritance pattern.

BP3.name: In-frame deletion/insertion in a repetitive region
BP3.description: In-frame deletions/insertions in a repetitive region without a known function.

BP4.name: Multiple computational tools predict no impact
BP4.description: Multiple lines of computational evidence suggest no impact on gene or gene product.

BP5.name: Variant found in a case with an alternate molecular basis
BP5.description: Variant found in a case with an alternate molecular basis for disease.

BP6.name: Reputable source reports variant as benign
BP6.description: Reputable source recently reports variant as benign, but evidence is not available to the laboratory to perform an independent evaluation.

BP7.name: Synonymous variant with no predicted splice impact
BP7.description: A synonymous (silent) variant for which splicing prediction algorithms predict no impact to the splice consensus sequence nor the creation of a new splice site.

classification_rules.pathogenic: PVS1 + >=1 PS, OR >=2 PS, OR PVS1 + >=2 PM, OR 1 PS + >=3 PP, OR >=2 PM + >=2 PP
classification_rules.likely_pathogenic: PVS1 + 1 PM, OR 1 PS + 1-2 PM, OR 1 PS + >=2 PP, OR >=3 PM, OR 2 PM + >=2 PP
classification_rules.uncertain_significance: Does not meet criteria for pathogenic/likely pathogenic or benign/likely benign
